options.why: Divided both by $2$ instead of the HCF $4$; $\frac{4}{6}$ is not fully simplified.
Divided the numerator by $4$ but the denominator by only $2$; both must be divided by the same number.
Simplified the numbers correctly but wrote the fraction upside down.
Divided only the numerator by $2$ and left the denominator unchanged.
Divided $16$ by $4$ and $6$ by $2$; both parts must be divided by the same factor.
Reduced to $\frac{8}{3}$ but then wrote it inverted.

questions.question_text: Which of these fractions is already fully simplified?

options.why: $\frac{6}{8}$ divides by $2$ to give $\frac{3}{4}$, so it is not fully simplified.
$\frac{4}{6}$ divides by $2$ to give $\frac{2}{3}$, so it can still be reduced.
$\frac{9}{12}$ divides by $3$ to give $\frac{3}{4}$, so it is not in simplest form.

questions.solution_text:
$5 \text{ and } 9 \text{ share no common factor except } 1$
$= \frac{5}{9}$

options.why: Divided both by $2$ only; $\frac{12}{18}$ still shares factors and is not fully simplified.
Divided both by $4$; $\frac{6}{9}$ still divides by $3$.
Divided both by $6$; $\frac{4}{6}$ still divides by $2$.
Divided both by $2$ once; $\frac{24}{36}$ still simplifies further.
Divided both by $8$; $\frac{6}{9}$ still divides by $3$.
Divided both by $12$; $\frac{4}{6}$ still divides by $2$.
